title: Minimal Expressions and Highest-Impact Equations — SuperBEST v5 (add=2n for ALL reals)

cost_model._obsolete: add_pos=3n and add_gen=11n are ELIMINATED. add=2n for ALL real x,y (ADD-T1). LEdiv+DEML path universally cheapest.

key_axiom.ADD_T1: add(x,y) = 2n for ALL real x,y. The old positive-domain path (EAL, add_pos=3n) is OBSOLETE. LEdiv(x,DEML(y,1)) costs 2n even for positive inputs.
key_axiom.implication: Sign-definiteness of operands NO LONGER drives cost. The central optimization question shifts from 'are summands positive?' to 'how many muls/divs are needed per term?'

task1_horner_method.title: Horner Polynomial Evaluation — The New Practical Method
task1_horner_method.description: Horner's method for p(x) = a_N*x^N + ... + a_1*x + a_0, written as (...((a_N*x + a_{N-1})*x + a_{N-2})*x + ... + a_0)

per_step.add_coeff: 2n (ALL reals, handles negative coefficients natively — no sign check)

task1_horner_method.cost_formula: 4N nodes for degree-N polynomial with leading coefficient != 1; 4(N-1) if leading coeff = 1 (free)

comparison_table.note: v5 direct: N*pow(3n) + N*mul for scale + (N-1)*add(2n) = 5N + 2N-2 = 7N-2
v5 Horner: N*(mul+add) = N*4 = 4N (uniform, no sign branching)
Horner wins at ALL N >= 1 in v5

specific_example_degree3.formula: 4*(N-1) = 4*3-2 = 10... correction: 4N with leading free = 4*3-0 = 12? No: N steps each 4n minus 2n for the free leading step = 4*3 - 2 = 10n. Verified.

verification.python_snippet:
def horner(coeffs, x):
    result = coeffs[0]
    for c in coeffs[1:]:
        result = result * x + c
    return result

coeffs = [1, -2, 3, -4]
assert horner(coeffs, 2) == 2   # x^3-2x^2+3x-4 at x=2: 8-8+6-4=2
assert horner(coeffs, 0) == -4  # constant term
assert horner(coeffs, 1) == -2  # 1-2+3-4=-2

task1_horner_method.v5_conclusion: In v5, Horner saves 3N-2 nodes over direct expansion for ALL N>=1, regardless of coefficient signs. For N=10: saves 28 nodes. The sign-branching overhead of v4 (Horner=13N for mixed vs Horner=5N for positive) is eliminated entirely.

task2_top10_most_impacted.title: Top-10 Equations by Node Savings (v4 add_gen=11n → v5 add=2n)
task2_top10_most_impacted.methodology: Savings per add_gen instance = 11n - 2n = 9n. Equations with N-1 add_gen operations save 9*(N-1). Table sorted by savings at representative N.

table.reason: Individual terms p(x,y)*ln(p(x,y)/(p(x)p(y))) are mixed-sign; forced add_gen for all cells
Terms p_i*ln(p_i/q_i) are mixed-sign (can be positive or negative); global KL>=0 but per-term sign is unknown
All ln(p_i) values are <= 0 (negative), sum is negative; add_gen required in v4 since operands are not positive
When outcomes x_i can be negative (losses/gains), terms x_i*P_i are mixed-sign; add_gen required in v4

table.savings_formula: 8*(N-1)  [per-period cost also changes: add_pos(1,r)=3n -> add(1,r)=2n, -1n per period]
table.reason: Mixed CF_t (investments and returns): sum requires add_gen for signed discounted cash flows. Also add_pos(1,r) -> add(1,r) saves 1n per period.
Mixture step (p_i+q_i)/2 used add_pos=3n (v4); each Shannon eval saves N-1 nodes from add_pos->add; total ~N+3*(N-1) savings

table.equation: Shannon Entropy H(X) — v5 optimal strategy
table.new_cost: 5N  (direct-sum strategy: compute p*ln(p), sum, then negate)
table.add_gen_count: N - 1  (add_pos in v4, now add=2n, enables strategy shift)
table.reason: In v4 neg-first strategy gave add_pos(3n); now add=2n means direct sum (3N nodes) + outer neg = 3N+2N-2+2 = 5N is cheaper than neg-first (7N-2) for N>=3

table.equation: Reaction Free Energy dG = dG_std + RT*ln(Q)
table.reason: RT*ln(Q) can be positive or negative depending on Q vs 1; forced add_gen=11n for the single addition dG_std + RT*ln(Q)
log10([A-]/[HA]) < 0 when [A-] < [HA]; forced add_gen=11n for pKa + log10_term. Now add=2n, both cases (acidic/basic buffer) cost 7n.

When rho < 0, alpha*x^rho can be very large but sign is positive; (1-alpha)*y^rho is positive too, BUT the CES paper incorrectly required add_gen when rho<0. With add=2n, savings=8n (was add_gen=11n, now add=2n, also saves 1n from add_pos->add).

task2_top10_most_impacted.note: Rankings 2-4 are tied at 81n savings for N=10. Ranking is by N=10 savings; MI ranks 1st at any N because its N is |X||Y| which grows quadratically.

task3_softmax_recomputation.title: Softmax Full Recomputation (N classes)

cost_full_softmax_all_outputs.strategy: Share denominator: compute N exps once, sum once, then N divisions

breakdown_v5.step: N div(exp_i, sum) for each output

cost_full_softmax_all_outputs.discrepancy_note: Wait: v4 cost should be higher. Let me recount: v4 denominator = N + 3(N-1) = 4N-3. Numerator exps already included. Per-output div = 2n each, N outputs = 2N. Total v4 = (4N-3) + 2N = 6N-3. Total v5 = (3N-2) + 2N = 5N-2.

verification.cost_model_check: N=3: v5=5*3-2=13n. Breakdown: 3 exp + 2 add + 3 div = 3+4+6=13. Correct.

task3_softmax_recomputation.note_on_prior_catalog: comp_all_domain2_summary.json lists 'partition function = 6N-3' — that formula was denominator-only (N exp + (N-1)*add_pos + 0 div). The 6N-3 v4 entry is ALSO the full softmax v4 total. The two interpretations coincide.

task4_new_constructions.title: New Constructions Enabled by Cheap add=2n

log_sum_exp.name: Log-Sum-Exp (multi-term)
log_sum_exp.v5_change: UNCHANGED — LEAd was already 1n in v5. Independent of add cost model.
log_sum_exp.note: For arbitrary positive b: LSE still uses LEAd internally. Multi-term: chain LEAd calls.

bregman_divergence.name: KL Divergence as Bregman Divergence (F = negative entropy)
bregman_divergence.note: KL = Bregman with F=negative entropy. The savings (16N-11 -> 7N-2) are entirely from add_gen -> add.

multivariate_gaussian_exponent_2d.name: Multivariate Gaussian Exponent (2D, diagonal Sigma)

breakdown.note: v5: add=2n; was add_pos=3n (both components are positive squared-term ratios)

multivariate_gaussian_exponent_2d.savings: 1n (from add_pos=3n -> add=2n for the sum of two positive components)

lennard_jones_potential.name: Lennard-Jones Potential V(r) = 4e*((s/r)^12 - (s/r)^6)
lennard_jones_potential.savings: 0n — Lennard-Jones uses sub (not add_gen); unchanged by ADD-T1
lennard_jones_potential.note: sub=2n was already optimal in v4. No benefit from ADD-T1 here.

horner_polynomial_v5.name: Horner Polynomial Evaluation (recap)
horner_polynomial_v5.key_insight: In v5, there is no longer any reason to choose different Horner implementations based on coefficient signs. One uniform implementation handles all cases optimally.

task5_minimal_expression_changes.title: Expressions with Significant Cost Changes in v5

changed.expression: sum of N signed real values
changed.note: The single biggest change. Any sum that was forced to use add_gen is now 2n.

changed.expression: sum of N positive real values
changed.note: Even positive sums benefit: 1n savings per addition.

changed.v5_cost: 5N  [direct: sum p*ln(p) then negate outer; no restructuring needed]
changed.note: The neg-first strategy is no longer optimal for N>=3. Direct sum (3N) + sum-reduction (2*(N-1)) + neg(2) = 5N.

unchanged.note: LEAd was already 1n; independent of add cost model
Uses sub not add; sub=2n was already correct in v4
Saves 1n: was add_pos=3n, now add=2n. Michaelis-Menten IS improved by ADD-T1 (listed here as a correction to the catalog entry which showed 7n).
sub=2n was already the cost; ADD-T1 does not affect subtraction

softmax_cost_correction.note: Corrected from task description. Full softmax for N classes:
softmax_cost_correction.breakdown_v5: N exp(1n each) + (N-1) add(2n each) + N div(2n each) = N + 2N-2 + 2N = 5N - 2
softmax_cost_correction.breakdown_v4: N exp(1n each) + (N-1) add_pos(3n each) + N div(2n each) = N + 3N-3 + 2N = 6N - 3

add_pos_eal_obsolete.title: Positive-Domain Addition Path (EAL, add_pos=3n) — OBSOLETE in v5
add_pos_eal_obsolete.statement: The EAL(EXL(0,x), EML(y,1)) path for add_pos cost 3n in v4. In v5, LEdiv(x, DEML(y,1)) = add(x,y) = 2n for ALL real x,y. There is no reason to use the EAL path for ANY addition.
consequence: All positive-domain sum optimizations (Michaelis-Menten, logistic sigmoid denominator, Fermi-Dirac, partition function sums) now cost 2n per add instead of 3n.
The 8n gap between add_gen and add_pos is ELIMINATED. A single add operator handles all cases at 2n.
Code that previously required sign analysis to choose add_pos vs add_gen is simplified: always use add=2n.

verification_summary.horner_p2: p(2) = 8 - 8 + 6 - 4 = 2. Verified by both Horner and direct evaluation.
cost_arithmetic: Horner degree-3 with leading 1: 5 steps * 2n each (add+mul per step, leading step free) = 10n. Verified.
Softmax N=10: 5*10-2 = 48n. Breakdown: 10 exp + 9*2n add + 10*2n div = 10+18+20=48. Verified.
